remember: Students will be able to recall and list the different types of databases.
Students will be able to explain the architecture of a DBMS.
Students will be able to identify the components of a database system.
Students will be able to list the types of database models.
understand: Students will be able to describe the purpose of a database system.
Students will be able to explain how data is organized in a database.
Students will be able to describe the difference between a relation and an attribute.
Students will be able to explain the concept of normalization in database design.
apply: Students will be able to design a simple database schema using ER model.
Students will be able to apply normalization rules to a given relation.
Students will be able to create a database using a DBMS and perform basic operations like insertion, deletion, and updating of data.
analyze: Students will be able to analyze the performance of a database system.
Students will be able to evaluate the design of a database schema for normalization and indexing.
Students will be able to compare and contrast different database models.

ncert_objectives: Explain the concept of a DBMS and its components.
Describe the architecture of a DBMS.
Explain the different types of database models.
Discuss the importance of data normalization in database design.
board_exam_objectives: Define DBMS and explain its components.
Explain the types of database models and their uses.
Discuss the importance of normalization in database design and explain the different types of normalization.
